data.content_ref: inline:The mechanism claims a pandemic or poor harvests 'disproportionately affected the elite due to their reliance on stored, rat-infested grain, while simultaneously increasing labor demands on the surviving underclass,' sparking a coordinated multi-regional uprising. This causal sequence is internally inconsistent: if the elite were disproportionately killed by disease from stored grain, they would be weakened and less able to increase extraction from the underclass, not more. The account needs the elite to simultaneously be decimated by disease AND intensify labor demands to provoke revolt, but the former undermines the capacity for the latter. The trigger and the revolt mechanism thus work at cross-purposes.
